prompt_l1: A diagram of the solar system, planets orbiting the sun in orderly elliptical paths.

ap_l2: Planets moving away/scattering; no orbits; chaotic expansion.

prompt_l1: A close-up of the Sun, a perfect glowing sphere of hot plasma.
ap_l1: Spherical shape; contained plasma.
prompt_l2: Gravity becomes repulsive. The Sun is exploding and dispersing into a shapeless cloud of gas, losing its spherical form.
prompt_l3: A close-up of the Sun in a universe where gravity acts as a repulsive force.
ap_l2: Sun expanding/exploding; loss of spherical shape; gas cloud.

prompt_l1: A starry night sky, mostly black with scattered twinkling stars.

ap_l2: Bright white sky; no darkness; blinding light.

prompt_l2: The speed of light is only 100 km/h. A sports car driving towards the camera appears blue (blueshift), while the back appears red.

ap_l2: Color distortion; blue tint on approaching object.

prompt_l1: A solid gold bar sitting on a velvet table.

prompt_l2: The strong nuclear force vanishes. The gold bar disintegrates instantly into a mist of hydrogen gas/protons as the nuclei fall apart.
prompt_l3: A gold bar on a table at the exact moment the strong nuclear force ceases to exist.
ap_l2: Disintegration; turning into mist/dust; loss of solidity.

prompt_l1: The Milky Way galaxy, a beautiful spiral disk with swirling arms.
ap_l1: Spiral shape; flat disk structure.
prompt_l2: Angular momentum is not conserved. The spiral arms disappear, and all stars are falling directly into the bright center of the galaxy.
prompt_l3: The Milky Way galaxy in a universe where angular momentum conservation does not exist.
ap_l2: Stars falling to center; loss of spiral shape; spherical collapse.

prompt_l2: Draw the interior of a black hole, but assume light and information can travel faster than the speed of light. This allows signals to escape the gravitational pull, revealing the matter inside.
ap_l2: The matter inside a black hole can be observed.
prompt_l3: Draw the interior of a black hole, but assume light and information can travel faster than the speed of light.

prompt_l1: Draw a view of a black hole, specifically focusing on the matter located inside the Schwarzschild radius (event horizon).
ap_l1: The matter inside a black hole cannot be seen.
prompt_l2: Draw the interior of a black hole, but assume information can be transmitted from inside the event horizon to the outside. This transmission makes the internal matter observable to us.
ap_l2: The matter inside a black hole can be observed.
prompt_l3: Draw the interior of a black hole, but assume information can be transmitted from inside the event horizon to the outside.

prompt_l1: A comet approaching the sun. Long exposure photography. The motion blur trail is very long and bright near the sun (fast speed) and short/dim far away.
ap_l1: Light trail is longer near the sun
prompt_l2: A universe where Kepler's Second Law is reversed. Long exposure photography.A comet approaches the sun but slows down significantly due to 'anti-gravity braking'. The motion blur trail is extremely short near the sun (slow speed) and very long far away.
ap_l2: Light trail is shorter near the sun
prompt_l3: A comet approaching a sun in a universe where gravity acts as a brake, causing objects to decelerate as they get closer to the massive center. Long exposure photography.

prompt_l1: Top-down view of a solar system. Inner planets have long motion trails (fast), outer planets have short motion trails (slow), following Kepler's 3rd Law.
ap_l1: Inner trails are longer than outer trails
prompt_l2: If orbital velocity is directly proportional to the distance from the center, draw the motion trails of the planets in the solar system, showing longer trails for outer planets.
ap_l2: Outer trails are longer than inner trails
prompt_l3: If orbital velocity is directly proportional to the distance from the center, draw the motion trails of the planets in the solar system.

prompt_l1: A planet in a stable orbit. It traces the same path repeatedly over time.
ap_l1: Path forms a closed loop
prompt_l2: If inertia doesn't exist, draw the orbital trajectory of a planet orbiting a star, showing it spiraling inward rapidly towards the sun.
ap_l2: Path is a spiral into the center
prompt_l3: If momentum cannot be maintained against gravity due to a lack of inertia, draw the orbital trajectory of a planet orbiting a star.

prompt_l1: A diagram of the solar system showing all planets orbiting on roughly the same flat plane (ecliptic).
ap_l1: Planets aligned on a flat plane
prompt_l2: If the conservation of angular momentum is broken, draw a solar system where planets orbit in a chaotic spherical tangle, moving in all directions.
ap_l2: Planets scattered in a sphere/tangle
prompt_l3: If the conservation of angular momentum is absent allowing planets to orbit in any random inclination around the core, draw a solar system.

prompt_l1: A gas giant planet. It is an oblate spheroid (bulges at equator) due to rotation.
ap_l1: Planet bulges outward at equator
prompt_l2: If rotation causes contraction, draw a gas giant that is pinched in the middle like an hourglass.
ap_l2: Planet is pinched inward at equator
prompt_l3: If rotation causes the equator to contract inward instead of bulging outward, draw a gas giant.

prompt_l1: Gravitational Redshift. Light emitted from near a black hole turns red as it climbs out of the gravity well.
ap_l1: Light turns red
prompt_l2: If gravity causes blueshift, draw light beams escaping a black hole turning bright blue and violet.
ap_l2: Light turns blue/violet
prompt_l3: If photons gain energy instead of losing it when climbing out of a gravitational potential, draw light escaping a black hole.

prompt_l1: Draw parallel light beams travelling through empty, flat space. They remain parallel.
ap_l1: The light beams remain parallel and keep the same distance.
prompt_l2: If space has negative curvature (Hyperbolic), draw parallel light beams diverging away from each other as they travel.
ap_l2: The light beams diverge and spread apart from each other.
prompt_l3: If space has negative curvature (Hyperbolic), draw two initially parallel light beams.

prompt_l1: A neutron star. A tiny, incredibly smooth sphere due to immense gravity.
ap_l1: Smooth sphere
prompt_l2: If gravity allows extreme mountains, draw a neutron star covered in massive, sharp spikes and tall jagged mountains.
ap_l2: Spiky/Mountainous surface
prompt_l3: If the crust of a neutron star can support massive topographic deviations despite gravity, draw a neutron star.

prompt_l1: An accretion disk around a black hole. It is a flat, swirling disk of matter.
ap_l1: Flat disk
prompt_l2: If angular momentum is not conserved, draw the accretion matter forming a perfect sphere cloud surrounding the black hole.
ap_l2: Spherical cloud
prompt_l3: If angular momentum is not conserved, draw a black hole feeding on matter.
